place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: This is a large swimming complex and great for kids on the 5th floor. There are 3 pools. One for small kiddies, one intermediate and one advanced. Pool is not heated but good for warm weather days. The Olympic pool has a shelter. There is an activesg gym on the 6th floor that overlooks the pool if you want to work out (separate entrance fee of 2.50 sgd). However, the gym are strict with footwear and need to bring your own towel.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Came here for my father's birthday. The food here was decent, but the price was definitely at the higher end. I had the ribeye steak (220g). The steak was tender, but was definitely too salty for my liking. Might be because of the chimichuri, so im not sure. Fries were nicely seasoned and thickly cut. The duck was nice, went well with the risotto. I also tried the ribs, which were nicely seasoned. The cauliflower were really good. Calamari rings were nice, but wasnt really very crispy. Cheesecake was really good, with a hint of saltiness and sweetness.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Whisk & Paddle is a fantastic spot with a great atmosphere and both indoor and outdoor seating. The view of the river and surrounding nature made for a truly relaxing dinner. The food was good, but the seabass was definitely the highlight and the best dish we ordered. It’s a bit on the pricier side, but the experience was worth the trip!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Nice restaurant with option for indoor vs outdoor seating. This us my 5th  time here and was not disappointed with the breakfast!!  Food was delicious and service was prompt & staff friendly.
Usually you have to wait for a table but we were lucky it was not crowded probably due to the June holidays.
A great place to hang out with friends over food & drinks.
Not very easily accessible if you don’t drive.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Wonderfully executed duck confit on risotto, brussels sprouts could be more caramelised or cooked. Mac and cheese tasty but a bit too wet, could be drier and cheesier. Wagyu beef burger was alright, fries not as fresh as they looked. Iced matcha latte was watered done after ice melted. Iced coffee was a small cup. Vanilla milkshake was delectable and sizeable, the best drink at the table. Prompt and adequate service. Complimentary water served. Place is huge, with lots of natural light. There's a small sheltered play area outdoors for kids. A little overpriced but a nice place to chill for an afternoon with its wide selection of drinks, mains, and desserts.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Visited Whisk & Paddle for the first time and was pleasantly surprised. The food was reasonably good—flavorful and satisfying without being over the top. Staff were warm and welcoming, which made the whole experience feel relaxed and easy.

It’s a great spot for families too. There’s a dedicated area for kids to play, which keeps them entertained while the adults enjoy their meal. The outdoor seating is a nice touch, especially if you enjoy dining with a view or a bit of fresh air.

Overall, a comfortable and friendly place worth checking out—especially if you're looking for a casual meal with good vibes.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Just next to a bus station, LRT and Waterway Point. Very useful clean and efficient way of travelling for all Punggo residents.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Super station. In and out very fast. Connected to the LRT and also the bus station in Punggol. Plus next to Waterway Point. Superb.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: On Friday the 13th in September 2019, I took C751A 7007/7008 to the station to use exit C to the bus interchange. I noticed the poster of Express 43e which runs between Punggol and Marine Parade Road operated by Go-Ahead Singapore from Loyang Bus Depot (LYDEP) using Mercedes-Benz O530 Citaros and MAN NL323F A22s. The bus that I took on Express 43e was SBS6454K, a Mercedes-Benz O530 Citaro Batch 2.

review_text: The park itself is fine if you don't intend to go onto the other side of the waterway because the number of bridges linking the two sides are few and far apart. I am not sure why it is designed like that. We happened to be on the opposite side of the "main attractions" like Waterway Point, SAFRA, etc and we just could not get over easily. I guess if you are aiming to have lots of steps, this is the place to be.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Quiet, underrated gem in the northeast. Punggol Park has a really chill vibe, especially around the big lake in the middle. Great for evening walks, light jogs, or just sitting down to unwind after a long day.

Nice mix of facilities too. There’s a Tenderbest nearby for easy bites, a gym pod if you want a quick workout, plus water coolers and vending machines around for convenience. You’ll also spot exercise stations catered for the elderly, which is a thoughtful touch. There is a nice woodball corner as well and also an open area for football.

Plenty of shaded paths and open spaces, so it never feels too crowded. You’ll see families, cyclists, and even people fishing by the pond. Simple, not overly fancy, but that’s part of the charm.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
If u are in Hougang / Sengkang area, and wanted a park to stroll or exercise.  Try this Punggol Park.
Pls note that although it called Punggol Park, it’s actually in end of upper Serangoon, Hougang area.
It’s a smaller to Bedok Reservior Park, but it’s wide enough for most outdoor exercise.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: We had an amazing family day out at SAFRA Punggol, especially at the KidzAmaze waterpark! This place is a paradise for children with its creatively designed water play areas and slides that are both safe and exciting. The facilities are well-maintained, and the staff are incredibly friendly and attentive, ensuring that all kids have a fun and safe experience. It's the perfect spot to cool down and allow the kids to burn off some energy in a fun-filled environment. Highly recommend for families looking for a great way to spend the day!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: One stop for some food and entertainment. Indoor water playground to bowling and coffee shop to McDonald’s it’s a good way to spend some quality time with kids. Sometimes carnival for kids in ground floor as well

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: We had a great time with my little one ( less than 1 year old ) . This place is great for 1-2 hrs of splashing entertainment. Very clean and hygienic pool and slides . The bathrooms and storage space was really well kept ( hot water - soap - quick availability) really made our time very smooth. We had a minor issue with being unable to book our tickets online ( for some reason the payment link was not visible and the rate cards online are not very accurate ) they have separate costs for members and non members ( 22$ for non members) makes sense to buy the membership card to save up. The kids love it. On Friday we had manageable crowd around. Quick bite at McDonald’s which is next door is a good option for closeby eats. Highly recommend ( carry your own water / toiletries )  for an easy experience.

review_text: A considerable amount of restaurants choices, but more fit to go to when you're looking for a place to enjoy sports and similar activities.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: One stop for some food and entertainment. Indoor water playground to bowling and coffee shop to McDonald’s it’s a good way to spend some quality time with kids. Sometimes carnival for kids in ground floor as well

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: We had a great time with my little one ( less than 1 year old ) . This place is great for 1-2 hrs of splashing entertainment. Very clean and hygienic pool and slides . The bathrooms and storage space was really well kept ( hot water - soap - quick availability) really made our time very smooth. We had a minor issue with being unable to book our tickets online ( for some reason the payment link was not visible and the rate cards online are not very accurate ) they have separate costs for members and non members ( 22$ for non members) makes sense to buy the membership card to save up. The kids love it. On Friday we had manageable crowd around. Quick bite at McDonald’s which is next door is a good option for closeby eats. Highly recommend ( carry your own water / toiletries )  for an easy experience.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: A considerable amount of restaurants choices, but more fit to go to when you're looking for a place to enjoy sports and similar activities.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Mamma Mia Trattoria E Caffè (Waterway Point)
review_text: Mammia Mia Trattoria E Caffe is truly a hidden gem for my family! Last time we did not even bother looking at this restaurant but because we decided to try this today, we will definitely come back to eat again. The food came at an acceptable amount of time and it was worth the wait! The pasta was moist and flavourful and the eggplant parmigiana was extremely cheesy, not to mention the tiramisu was absolutely phenomenal. The atmosphere was energetic with loud booming music and the lighting was truly a vibe. Staff was kind and friendly and attentive to our orders and was quick to act. Overall a good place to have dinner, 5/5 stars!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Mamma Mia Trattoria E Caffè (Waterway Point)
review_text: Wow! Pleasantly surprised by the taste and quality of the food here. I ordered the Minestrone Soup,0Milanese Chicken with Creamy Spiced Pasta, Chilli Crab Risotto and Cheese, Pep Mafalda, Eggplant Parmigiana and Tiramisu. Waiting to try the Tiramisu as I write this.  All the food is very tasty and well cooked and presented. Happy to try this place out!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Mamma Mia Trattoria E Caffè (Waterway Point)
review_text: Staff here were really kind to my kids which made the dining experience so much better. The food portion was generous, service was efficient. They gave my kids biscuits after they were done with their food as a little distraction while the adults finish their foods.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Mamma Mia Trattoria E Caffè (Waterway Point)
review_text: We were kindly served by Sulaiman, who was very friendly and prompt in his services. Truly appreciated the welcoming and cosy atmosphere created by the team at the Waterway Point branch as all them wished us goodbyes as we left. More importantly, the food blew all of us away and we ended up ordering more and more even though we were stuffed because the dishes are all just so well-made. We had the crunchy, well-seasoned calamari, the pan-seared chicken with THE BEST MASH POTATOES, sea bass, squid ink pasta, beef bolognese pasta and the fries. We ended the meal with a plate of tiramisu. Each order was a complete hit with our family. It was such a lovely experience dining out here with the whole family.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Mamma Mia Trattoria E Caffè (Waterway Point)
review_text: Enjoyed our meal at Mamma Mia Trattoria e Caffè at Waterway Point. The truffle mushroom risotto was rich and comforting, and the carbonara was creamy with a generous topping of crispy bacon. Loved the bruschetta trio — especially the tuna and egg salad toppings. The fried chicken wings were nicely seasoned and crispy too. Great ambience, friendly service, and well-presented dishes. A lovely spot for casual Italian dining.

I mean seriously the mall is so big n spacious but there is no any Indian food in it or at least a Muslim food on it ... Very disappointed ☹️ whenever I go waterway point the only options I get to eat is either fast food or Milo toast ...there r restaurants like buddy hoggies n han's ... But seriously if I want to enjoy a nice meal I have to spend so much money??? Can't I get to enjoy a nice meal for a cheaper price at a normal foodcourt???  There r take away shops like stuff'd .... Kebab, burrito, burrito bowl used to cost much cheaper than the price is costing now ... Just increased the price for kebab, burrito n burrito bowls ... N no dine in options from last time till now ... I felt not worth buying from this shop ... But I always c a long queue in this shop ... ☹️ There is a shop which similar like stuff'd...
Guzman y gormez which also sells kebab n burritos ... it has dine in options... BUT VERY EXPENSIVE... have tried there before ... Did not went back to that shop again 🤫 ... Oh n I just remembered... Waterway point has waffles.... For any1 who is a sweet tooth ... 😁 Many flavored drinks like luckin coffee ... N boost ... There r many flavored drinks shops in waterway point but not healthy... Too much sugar... I don't really encourage people to drink all this flavored drinks...
Whatever age anybody is or whether people with diabetes or not too much sugar intake n too much fast foods intake NOT HEALTHY...
Another thing which I want to tell everyone is that when eating at McDonalds please do not give your hp to any1 in the McDonald's... I have this prob with McDonalds whenever I m eating McDonalds... Since I eat at waterway McDonalds today n this prob happened again I wish to tell everyone... The person who is wearing the yellow shirt in the McDonald's I guess she is the manager's assistant she asked for my phone to do McDonalds feedback...I did not want to be rude so gave hery HP ... Customers r supposed to do the feedback not any1 else ... The McDonald's was so dirty ... I had to wipe the table before I eat ... Fly was flying around... I want to feedback but cannot bec she do feedback... The feedback she did from my phone was submitted under my name but I did do the feedback... Just bec they want to increase their outlet rating they do feedback from customers phone ... Everything they put 5 star ... Taking a customer's phone n doing feedback is very wrong ... As a customer I can make a complaint as this has happened many times ... But I just decided to just leave it ... Next time I go McDonalds when again ask for my phone I m strictly going to say as a customer I will do feedback ... I guess I have to be rude at times ... M being too nice ... Peo taking advantage... I don't want the same thing happening to any1 else so m telling every1...

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
This mall has everything you need for a relaxing weekend. There is a kids’ play area, as well as a jogging track with a beautiful lake view.

It offers plenty of dining options, including Din Tai Fung, Song Fa Bak Kut Teh, and many others. For tea lovers, there are popular choices such as CHICHA San Chen and CHAGEE. If you’re looking for dessert, Henri Charpentier and Mister Donut are great options. Coffee lovers can also grab a drink at Starbucks.

The mall also has a supermarket, making it a convenient one-stop destination for shopping, dining, and leisure. It’s a perfect place to spend the weekend, especially if you prefer not to travel to the city centre.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
I went to Punggol by MRT and it is very spacious bus interchange with modern facilities.If you need to go to the City area, then of course, you need to take MRT.

It is very clean bus and MRT interchange including the facilities like Toilet.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Very useful and efficient bus station. Can be slightly unsafe at the opening, with some bad accidents involving deaths to pedestrians. Be very careful crossing the roads here.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Bus don’t stop even after bell was press

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Spacious bus interchange that you can reach easily from the MRT. Connects you to the rest of Punggol area. Take bus 84 to go to Coney Island. Make sure you take Exit B from the MRT to reach this place.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: It is a great place to spend time with family and friends. It is new, spacious, and has many facilities, including McDonald's, Punggol library, and a food court at Level 2. The library offers various activities and has a wide selection of books. There are also running and cycling paths, fitness areas, and community events for people of all ages.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: One Punggol is a new building that is huge, well located, has a nice food court, a spacious multipurpose hall, a wide ground floor for viewing shows on a big screen, and has an excellent car park. Just beside Punggol Waterway and a short walk from other places. Love there’s a Blood Bank here too. Thank you 🙏🏻

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Good airy, spacious hawker centre with many variety of food.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Came here on 22-10-25 evening for dinner.  Most store were closed, went round and decided to try this HKM which recommended by Google map. I ordered the $6 which came with 3 prawns, 2 small ring of sotong and few very small dice of pork belly. This is somewhat the dry type. Nothing special not worth the price but cab eat lah.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: An office here where one can get help for government services. It's especially good for old folks to get help. My favourite part of this place is the Punggol Regional Library. It has a vast no number of books and seating areas for browsing books. Lots of computers to access the internet as well. Many students go to the study area to study. A very condusive area for students. However, the food sold at the food court needs improvement. Lots of car park in the basement.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Dear Management Team,
I am writing to share a truly exceptional experience I recently had at your store.
While shopping for meat at GIANT Food, I was warmly welcomed by an outstanding employee whose name tag identified him as Mr. Youn Sang. From the moment I approached the counter, he made my entire experience feel special and valued.
What impressed me most was his attentiveness to everyone around him. No customer in line was left unattended. Mothers with children were acknowledged with patience and kindness, and every single person received a warm, personalized greeting. His professionalism, positive energy, and genuine care for customers truly stood out.
It is rare to encounter someone who takes such pride in delivering exceptional service. Mr. Youn Sang made my shopping experience not only pleasant but memorable, and I felt it was important that your management team be made aware of the remarkable job he is doing.
Please extend my sincere appreciation to him. Employees like Mr. Youn Sang are a tremendous asset to your organization.
Sincerely,
Jay.C

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Worst hypermarket experience. Only three checkout counters were open on a Saturday night, which naturally led to long queues. We waited about 40 minutes just to check out. If the store is short-staffed, it should reconsider operating 24 hours. Giant, please learn from Sheng Siong.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This is one of the best mega warehouses in Singapore to shop on the East side as it carries many brands of food, household n electrical items . The layout is good where one can shop in comfort. There is a food court that has a nice range of food where one can indulge after the shopping. A great place to stock up essentials for home as the ample parking is convenient to trolley ones purchase to the car. One can find wonderful bargains as the store holds promotional product brands that are a steal. Happy shopping , happy family.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Today, January 12, 2026, at the Giant food court, I ordered *mee hoon* soto from the Nasi Padang stall, asking for an extra chicken add-on for $2.00. After I collected the food, it was not hot and contained only about one tablespoon of chicken. I asked the lady, who sounded Indonesian, about it. She was not happy when I asked her if the $2.00 chicken had been added. She pulled the bowl, and not wanting to create a scene, I just pulled it back and left. I'm very disappointed with her attitude; at the very least, she could have explained nicely. This will be the last time.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Final Giant Hypemarket at Tampines ✔️✔️✔️✔️✔️👍👍👍

Giant Hypermarket at Tampines is ideal for one-stop grocery needs if you live in this area. There is a wide range of electronics, kitchenware, groceries, a semi-wet market, fruits, and vegetables. The prices are reasonable. There are also food stalls serving plenty of local dishes. Halal and vegetarian options are available and typically served until around 9:30 PM. Additionally, there is ample parking space with free parking available. If you live in this area, it’s an excellent place to shop for your daily needs.
Chris
17 Sept 2025 11am

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
AMK Hub is one of the most convenient and practical malls in Ang Mo Kio. The location is excellent, as it is directly connected to Ang Mo Kio MRT station and the bus interchange, making it very easy to reach by public transport.

The mall has a good mix of shops, dining options, supermarkets, daily essentials, and services. It is suitable for families, students, office workers, and residents living nearby. Whether you are looking for groceries, a quick meal, shopping, banking services, or just a place to walk around, AMK Hub has almost everything you need in one location.

I like that the mall is very accessible and easy to navigate. There are many food choices, from fast food and takeaway options to cafés and restaurants for proper meals. It is also a convenient place to run errands, meet friends, or spend time with family.

Although the mall can get quite crowded during weekends and meal times, the overall environment is still lively, clean, and well maintained. The air-conditioning is comfortable, and the shops are arranged in a way that makes it easy to move around.

Overall, AMK Hub is a reliable and convenient neighbourhood mall with a good variety of food, shopping, and essential services. It is definitely one of the best places to visit in Ang Mo Kio for everyday needs, meals, and casual shopping.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: AMK Hub is a really convenient neighbourhood mall, especially since it’s directly connected to Ang Mo Kio MRT and the bus interchange. It’s super easy to get to, which makes it a popular stop for quick meals or shopping. There’s a wide range of food options here—from food courts with local and international dishes to plenty of fast-food and restaurant chains. It’s not as exciting as the bigger malls, but it’s great for everyday needs and casual dining.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A good, classic neighbourhood mall with food options that leave you spoilt for choice. A rectangular-shaped mall with basements mostly eateries, Level 1 & 2 mostly shops, and Level 3 is mostly educational centres.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Went to AMK Hub for lunch. ate at that Malaysia Boleh. There are many varieties of food here suitable for your lunch or dinner...My hubby always eat the cray pot rice which comes with chicken and salted fish. That day we ate that fish ball black noodles. This store add some tasty dark sauce to their noodles which gives a different taste to it's noodles. Taste very yummy. I had their fried tang hoon. Same store...They have varies type of fried noodles which you must tried. Not bad too. We always end our meal with a bowl of Chendol which is classic with all Malaysia Boleh outlets...They definitely have this menu in all their outlets without fail...AMK Hub is filled with varieties type of food on their 3 floors esp basement 1 and basement 2...a full line of snacks and food for you to choose from. Try coming here for your meals...no regret....I have had enclosed photos of our meals that day...Enjoy!!!!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Lots of shops and less shopping shops.only essential shops are available like daily use stuffs. More favorite thing attracted me it the play area next to jollie bee food court

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
This is a seaside dining and lifestyle destination located at the northern tip of Punggol. The two-story building features a variety of restaurants with waterfront views:
Seafood Specialties: Famous for classic Singaporean seafood at House of Seafood and Ponggol Seafood.
Cuisines: Options include Thai food at Siam Square Mookata, Japanese at Izakaya 95, and local favorites like the White Restaurant (known for White Bee Hoon).
Bars & Cafes: Georges by the Bay provides a relaxed pub atmosphere.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: This Korean BBQ has plenty of performances to keep you entertained while waiting for the meat to be bbq by the staff. Service is quick and good.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Just had siblings reunion here and the atmosphere was good. Large crowds maybe it was on Saturday.

Food was tasty only it was not served together as in it was served 1 by 1 so we never get to eat it with the rice altogether.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: In the small island of Singapore, there are not many "village style" place for recreation, F&B and back to nature. This place goes to my "hang out with friends list"

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: This seafood restaurant is away from the city in Ponggol. But if you drive don’t give it amiss. The food is sumptuous and in good proportion. It was a treat for us but I suppose it is reasonably priced. The service is good and the the ambience is good if it was not for the small irritating flies. Then, it went off. If you a seafood lover, please visit this restaurant. 😊

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: In the small island of Singapore, there are not many "village style" place for recreation, F&B and back to nature. This place goes to my "hang out with friends list"

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: This seafood restaurant is away from the city in Ponggol. But if you drive don’t give it amiss. The food is sumptuous and in good proportion. It was a treat for us but I suppose it is reasonably priced. The service is good and the the ambience is good if it was not for the small irritating flies. Then, it went off. If you a seafood lover, please visit this restaurant. 😊

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
This is a seaside dining and lifestyle destination located at the northern tip of Punggol. The two-story building features a variety of restaurants with waterfront views:
Seafood Specialties: Famous for classic Singaporean seafood at House of Seafood and Ponggol Seafood.
Cuisines: Options include Thai food at Siam Square Mookata, Japanese at Izakaya 95, and local favorites like the White Restaurant (known for White Bee Hoon).
Bars & Cafes: Georges by the Bay provides a relaxed pub atmosphere.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This is a quiet green park in Singapore. Looks like an old park with lot of greenery. It's clam, quiet and with lot of shade its ideal for a relaxing time. The famous Fort canning tree tunnel is also located here. Entrance is free. Popular place amongst the tourists. Good place to relax.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Fort Canning Park is a lush green destination right in the heart of the city, notable for its tranquil atmosphere and historical significance. It's perfect for strolling, relaxing, and taking photos, especially with its famous "tree tunnel" viewpoint.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Visited in the late morning before the weather got too hot , historical park with heritage buildings & a gallery of history of this place was event fully refreshing .

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Hainanese curry rice: Fragrant curry, good portion (especially with the rice)
Mixed veg rice: Often a long queue in the evening. They have good fried rice, about $5+ for 1 veg+1 meat+fried rice. Ingredients are nice
Herbal soup: Average portion, nice lotus root soup (though, it tastes about the same in most herbal soup stalls), goes well with pumpkin rice.
Korean: Both Korean stalls are fine, Jin Kimchi in particular is a chain stall but the quality is good and the packaging is well sealed.
Butter and Cream: really good cakes and tarts, but I recommend eating fresh. Especially the egg tarts, they taste much better fresh than kept overnight in the fridge.
Carrot cake: white carrot cake is pretty good, but can be quite salty / oily. They forgot to add / remove chili based on request a few times...but overall the food is good and I will buy again
Drink stall: Teh peng is ok. Avocado drink is a scam, the gula melaka is very sweet and tastes good but I am fairly certain "avocado juice" should be blended...not have the whole avocado....

There's nothing in particular that stands out a lot, but it's relatively affordable in general and food is not bad! Just won't recommend the avocado "juice" 😹

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Big, convenient hawker centre near Sengkang MRT & Compass One offering a wide variety of local & international food at reasonable prices. Seating is spacious & breezy after renovations.

10% off when paying via the FairPrice app 👍

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
This 1 star is given to a Vietnamese Bánh mì stall.
It cost me SG$8 and the piece of chicken inside can't even been seen outside the bread. It's smaller than 旺旺米饼. When the lady pass the packet of Bánh mì to me, its feels like I am holding a empty bag. Don't waste your money on this stall. For SG$8, I can have a big piece of fried chicken for Economic Rice with another meat and vegi, a big burger, a 2 pieces fried chicken from western food stall, a whole chicken from Cold Storage with $0.10 less.
Photo shows that I have eaten half of the bread and the chicken is haven't appear yet.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Dear Owner of Kopitiam(Fairprice Group), may a humble common peasant like me ask, have your company staffs trained to handle the drinks stall with the standard operation procedure through out all the kopitiam outlet. If so, how do you justify the amount of drinks that is served to customer. $2.70 for this cup of milo, a cup full of ice (to the brim) and poured in with cold (ready made) milo, compared to outlets that serve the same thing, a cup full of ice and poured in with hot(freshly made) milo. And also given the recent inflation, does Fairprice Group find this price ($2.70) reasonable? Thank you.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: The stall that sells curry rice v generous. Can choose veggie ☺️. Prev tried the chicken chop. Today tried the fish fillet

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: An unforgettable family sports day where the children learned to be brave, work together, and participate actively. There were a wide variety of activities, and the kids had a great time.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: MindChamps Preschool has exceeded our expectations. The school places strong emphasis on confidence-building and critical thinking, which has really helped my child become more expressive and independent. The teachers are attentive and provide regular updates, so parents feel involved in the learning journey. The environment is safe, positive, and structured, yet fun. I appreciate how the school focuses not just on academics, but also on character development and emotional growth

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Recently enrolled to MindChamps Punggol. It's a great school with good facilities, nice and engaging teachers, condusive environment for learning.

They also frequently conduct workshops to let parents know what they are learning at school and how we are able to support them at home.

Update - December 2025:

Attended a Year-End Musical and Graduation concert for the first time. I was genuinely surprised by how GRAND it was! I was quite astonished to see the little Champs able to memorize such long lines, complex dance sequences, and choreography. It was easy to forget just how young they are!

The theme, "A Small Island - Little Hands, Big Dreams," was especially close to our hearts. It was truly heartwarming to watch my daughter, Evelyn, learn about Singapore and sing 'Home' so beautifully. She genuinely loves to perform, sing, and dance. I could clearly see her growth and increased confidence throughout this entire process.

A huge thank you to the entire team for teaching, organizing this incredible performance, and, of course, for taking such wonderful care of Evelyn and nurturing her growth ❤️

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Great preschool, My child feels comfortable and supported, and always comes home with excitement to share what they’ve learned. The school also places strong emphasis on values, discipline, and teamwork, which helps shape the children not just as students but as individuals.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: A strong foundation in language begins early in a child’s life, and as an educator, I firmly believe that parent workshops are a powerful way to nurture meaningful home-school learning connections. This is my third MRW workshop at MindChamps, and I am truly heartened by the genuine interest our parents show in their Champs' learning journey. Their enthusiasm and eagerness to be involved are not only encouraging but also play a vital role in building confident, capable and joyful learners.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
This is very great neighbourhood library with librarians who are very observant and go the extra mile. Especially I thank Ms Fazilah 9 Nov 2025 at 7.30pm. She goes the extra mile fuss free, efficient, responsive and responsible. She checked the return conveyor belt and took effort to accompany me downstairs to search shelf and all displays thoroughly. She is very detailed to get both call number and shelf number. She found the book at display within very short time and went back to her routine duty which impresses me with her work ethics.

Before Ms Fazilah helped me I tried to get the book as search shown available on shelf but cannot find. I approached another white hair lady who situated there. She gave up very fast after just finding from the usual numbered shelf which I told her I already done. Then just quickly dismissed me and gave me excuses impatiently that it may be on wrong shelf. This is although I explained I am very sure it should be somewhere in library because not many people in library and I saw no one using the book. So the previous staff did not help me much she did not even look at special display areas...

Thank you so much Sengkang library for having MS Fazilah such a rare, helpful exemplary staff who exhibits great time management and problem solving skill. I thank her for resolving patiently and quickly without excuse and not dismissing customer request.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
We recently took our little one to Sengkang Public Library, which is conveniently located right inside Compass One. It made for a really easy family outing, as we could grab a quick bite at the mall before heading up. The library itself is quite spacious and felt very clean and well-maintained, spread across two levels. Our child absolutely loved the Early Literacy Area; they have some really engaging displays and the book modules, designed like traditional fishing baskets, were a nice touch that caught our attention. It’s a bright and welcoming space, perfect for little hands to explore.

While the children's section can get a bit lively, as expected with so many excited kids, there are plenty of other quiet spots throughout the library if you need a calmer environment. My wife and I appreciated the wide selection of books for all ages, and I even spotted a dedicated 'Tween Space' which will be great for our child in a few years. There are ample seating areas, so it's easy to settle down with a stack of books and spend a good hour or two. It’s definitely a solid choice for a family afternoon out, combining learning with comfort.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
What I like in libraries: Books that are available that I originally have on my online To-Be-Read (TBR). Spacious environments. Multiple book genres.

The first thing I noticed that drew my attention when I walked in was this little Harry Potter castle, which was on top of a bookshelf. The second thing I noticed was that they had a huge selection of audiovisuals.

Once you walk a bit more towards the left, you’ll see a few bookshelves, and close to those bookshelves is a long white table. And near the white table there are 2 seats close to a window. I think that area is a really nice spot for reading, studying, and using your computers.

If you want straight, you’ll see comics, and a teen section. In between 2 bookshelves, there are 2 seats, for safe distancing. There is also a magazine section.

There is also a quick charge area. Near the quick charge area there are a few shelves of adult fiction.

Overall, I think the Sengkang Library is a nice library for spending a long duration of time with your computer, or having a book you want to read. You can also study in the area of the white table and 2 seats near the window.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: This is a two storey library and that easily makes it better. If you feel like the children section can be loud, you may find other reading spots. Conveniently located inside the mall with lots of reading books for small children. Lots of library staff too to remind the children to keep quiet while inside the library. My daughters love to hang out in this library after school.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Located within Compass One shopping mall, the library covers an area of 2,136 square meters, New areas in the library include the Early Literacy Area where families can read and bond together, the Tween Space targeted at children aged 10 to 14, and a dedicated Adults section for quiet reading.  It was officially opened on 30 November 2002, comprising of 2 levels - it is convenient and accessible in the shopping centre.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
One of the old malls that had been revamped.

We were here years ago and after coming back bout 15years later, the place looks really different!

Lets dive in!

Location:
Integrated with the bus interchange and mrt, this mall is considered a hub for residents living in woodlands. There are plenty of entrances and is easily accessible everywhere.

Amenities:
Other than a furniture shop, you can find almost everything here. We were in awe and the food selections that we can find here. Not forgetting the gadget shops that have competitive prices. They also have a prayer room, which we were glad to use.

Parking:
Despite the many accesible options around, this mall has lots of parking lots available., subjected to non-peak hours yeah😅. Alternatively, there are also other nearby areas to park actually.

All in all, if you are up for an exercise to walk, this is one place to head down to. After walking on all floors, treat yourself to shopping and a good meal too!

Highly recommended from us!

Cheers!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Explored Causeway Point in a day. Anchor tenants like Metro Store, Courts for electrical and furniture, Uniqlo Japanese fashion wear. Lots of F&B selections from Kopitiam Bagus halal food court, Food Republic food court, Chinese, Japanese, Korean & western cuisines including lots of fried chicken - Texas, KFC, Jollibee, 4Fingers , Chagee, and lots of BbTea shops. Bakeries & Patisserie like Swee Heng, Annabelle, rrooll. You can spend a whole day exploring beautiful home decor at Madera and kitchen needs. To pamper yourselves with a pair of new glasses or shades from the few optical stores as well as beauty salons for hair and nails. Nostalgic snacks store, automated machines for the SGFR viral snack, Popmart machines can be found too.  From Causeway Point basement it will lead you to Woods Square and Woodlands Civic Center. Highly recommended to visit.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Wow many eateries at the basement offering good food here. Also first floor have a lot of nice shops selling food for takeaway all in one shopping mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Causeway Point is one of the older heartland malls in Singapore. Located just outside Woodlands MRT station, which is now an interchange to TEL line, and a bus interchange, it is in a very strategic location which explains why it’s never not busy. Causeway Point is also connected to Woodlands Civic Center and Woods Square. Beside it is also a location for occasional temporary Pasar Malam.

So, if you don’t like people, this place can be hellish. On the other side, it’s also a very convenient place to get everything you need from grocery, stationery, household items, electronic, pet stuff, food, everything you need.

The mall has everything, from Cinema to Yakun, Popular bookstore to FairPrice supermarket. There are many food varieties to choose from including two food courts. There are Metro department store and Courts. There are pharmacies like Watson, Guardian, and Unity.

Pros: You can find almost everything you need.

Cons: People! So many people!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Love ❤️ Causeway Point.

It’s near my house 🏠 and it has a wide variety of stores under one roof. Satisfy all my needs.

My daughter and I often went there to eat.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
One of the old malls that had been revamped.

We were here years ago and after coming back bout 15years later, the place looks really different!

Lets dive in!

Location:
Integrated with the bus interchange and mrt, this mall is considered a hub for residents living in woodlands. There are plenty of entrances and is easily accessible everywhere.

Amenities:
Other than a furniture shop, you can find almost everything here. We were in awe and the food selections that we can find here. Not forgetting the gadget shops that have competitive prices. They also have a prayer room, which we were glad to use.

Parking:
Despite the many accesible options around, this mall has lots of parking lots available., subjected to non-peak hours yeah😅. Alternatively, there are also other nearby areas to park actually.

All in all, if you are up for an exercise to walk, this is one place to head down to. After walking on all floors, treat yourself to shopping and a good meal too!

Highly recommended from us!

Cheers!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Timbre+ One North is a great dining destination in Singapore's One North district, offering a vibrant atmosphere that appeals to a wide range of visitors. Whether you are meeting colleagues after work, catching up with friends, hosting a casual business discussion, or simply looking for a good meal, it provides a welcoming and comfortable setting.

One of its biggest strengths is the variety of food available. With options ranging from Indian and Chinese cuisine to continental dishes and local favourites, there is something to suit almost every taste and preference. This makes it an excellent choice for groups with diverse food interests.

The venue is clean, well organized, and efficiently managed, creating a pleasant dining experience even during busy periods. The lively yet relaxed environment makes it suitable for both working lunches and social gatherings.

Good food, reasonable prices, and a wide selection of cuisines make Timbre+ One North a place that offers excellent value for money and is well worth a visit when in the area.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Punggol Regional Library: A Visual Masterpiece in the North-East

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

As a photographer, I usually look for subjects behind the lens rather than books on a shelf, but the **Punggol Regional Library** at One Punggol is a rare exception where the architecture is as much a draw as the collection.

The Architecture & Light
From a design perspective, this isn't just a building; it’s a study in geometric lines and natural lighting. The massive glass facade allows for incredible "Golden Hour" shots if you’re positioned on the upper levels. The way the light spills across the reading tiers creates a beautiful interplay of highlights and shadows—perfect for those who enjoy **minimalist or architectural photography**.

Key Spots for Enthusiasts:
The Lens of Literature: This is the library’s "hero shot." The circular, tiered seating area provides a fantastic perspective for wide-angle frames, capturing the sheer scale of the 5-storey atrium.

The World and Us (Level 5): The curation here is top-notch, but the real prize is the outdoor terrace. You get a sweeping view of the Punggol skyline and the lush greenery surrounding the area—ideal for some urban landscape captures.
ExperienceIT & MakeIT (Level 4): For those who love macro or tech-focused shots, the 3D printers and robotics labs offer some great industrial-chic textures.

Inclusivity & Tech
Beyond the visuals, the library is a marvel of inclusive design. The **Calm Pods** and the **Borrow-n-Go** accessible walkways are thoughtfully integrated into the flow of the building. Even the way the shelves are spaced feels intentional, providing clear leading lines that make the space feel infinite.

Pro-Tips for Visitors:
Gear: Bring a wide-angle lens (16mm to 35mm equivalent) to truly capture the interior volume.
Timing: Try a weekday morning for "clean" shots without the crowds.
Post-Visit: You’re right next to the One Punggol Hawker Centre for a quick meal, and a short walk from the Waterway, which offers plenty of additional landscape opportunities along the river.

Whether you're there to research your next project or to capture the soul of modern Singaporean architecture, this place is a must-visit. 📸📚

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Overall, the library is pretty clean with many interesting facilities. What really made this a 4 star review rather than a 5 star review, is that the children's section is not to right place to read. On level 2 (children's section), i could hear kids screaming and stomping on the floor every second, so i advise to never study on level 2.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Punggol Library is an amazing space for readers and learners alike! With its fully automated system, it’s incredibly convenient to borrow and return books without the need for staff assistance. The library offers a wide range of books and digital resources, catering to various interests and age groups.

The environment is quiet, clean, and modern, making it perfect for studying, reading, or simply relaxing with a good book. The dedicated children’s section and interactive areas add a fun touch for families.

Its seamless self-service technology and thoughtfully designed spaces truly make it a library of the future. Highly recommended for anyone in Singapore looking for a productive or peaceful spot!

The Architecture & Light
From a design perspective, this isn't just a building; it’s a study in geometric lines and natural lighting. The massive glass facade allows for incredible "Golden Hour" shots if you’re positioned on the upper levels. The way the light spills across the reading tiers creates a beautiful interplay of highlights and shadows—perfect for those who enjoy **minimalist or architectural photography**.

The Lens of Literature: This is the library’s "hero shot." The circular, tiered seating area provides a fantastic perspective for wide-angle frames, capturing the sheer scale of the 5-storey atrium.

ExperienceIT & MakeIT (Level 4): For those who love macro or tech-focused shots, the 3D printers and robotics labs offer some great industrial-chic textures.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Chomp Chomp was established in 1972 as a "hawkers centre", now better known as foodcentre. It has been upgraded a few times over the years, the latest one was end of 2025 till March 2026. Our cleaning manager gave us the tip about the great food at the very recently reopened foodhall. And she was spot on, what an absolute treat, this hidden gem. A must visit, higly recommended.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Favourite hawker centre !! I have been a regular diner at Chomp Chomp since I was young. The standard still feels around the same. I usually get the sambal stingray, oyster omelette, satay, bbq wings and carrot cake. The seats inside the centre are sometimes stuffy but the ones outside and at the back are really nice. love love love

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Lots of local dishes to choose. After renovation the place is still SMALL. Come early if you like a good seat. Better ventilation with the huge fans. Some of the stalls are no longer there.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
The XXL sugarcane juice is the hit.
Other foods are average, some of them require fairly long waiting time.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Closed from 27 October 2025 to 26 January 2026 for renovations and upgrading works. This is a great food centre with a diverse selection of hawker food. Been eating here when i was younger and living in Serangoon Gardens. My current fav char kway teow is located at this food centre and they have quite a few good stalls serving BBQ sambal stingray.

On my last visit I was surprised to see that Chomp Chomp isn't as crowded as it used to be back in the day. On the bright side, it’s way easier to get a seat now.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Well built! The station is linked to the mall, too! I’ve took the newest train to Harbourfront, and it was AMAZING! The trains were silent! There were even screens displaying the next train and other safety rules as well. The onboard TV up the train will also play some interesting videos. Good!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Just want to share so next time you won't be confused. Exit A is going to the Waterway Point Mall, Exit B is the opposite of Bus Interchange, the road is along the Mall as well, and Exit C is going to bus interchange. I would like to rate their toilet also, even though there are a lot of people, they maintained it clean. Good Job.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: LRT is spacious but crowded during most of the times during the day. West loop trains are bit small in size ( 1 compartment ) compared to most east loop trains (2 compartments)

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: MRT Singapore is efficient vastly superior to what I experience back home very clean and the PA very clear and precise 4 language yes 4 once u get down just in front is a lift u don't have to walk long to get one . One thing I find puzzling is where is the dustbin and of course no public clock . If u have trash figure it out . Singapore is very lively u ses lot's of senior walking around and lots of them still working in Airport,  mall , MRT station and everywhere.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Punggol MRT Station is clean, spacious, and really well-organised. The interchange setup with the LRT makes transfers easy, and the signs are super clear so you won’t get lost even during busy hours. Trains come frequently, and the station staff are usually helpful when you need directions. The surrounding area is convenient too — Waterway Point is directly connected, so grabbing food or doing errands after your commute is very easy. Overall, a reliable and comfortable station to travel from.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
• Free entry & hours: Compass One is a public shopping mall, so entry is free. The mall’s main contact page lists opening hours as 10:00am–10:00pm daily, while individual stores may open earlier or close later depending on the tenant.

• What & why go: Compass One is a major Sengkang town-centre mall with 180+ brands and a wide mix of shopping, dining and everyday services. It is described by the mall as one of the area’s most extensive and fully integrated malls.

• Top features to see: You’ll find a mix of fashion, food, beauty, electronics and family-friendly outlets, plus anchors like Cold Storage, McDonald’s, Starbucks, POPULAR and the Sengkang Public Library inside the mall.

• Getting there: The mall is at 1 Sengkang Square, Singapore 545078. The official “Getting Here” page says to take the North East Line and alight at Sengkang Station (NE16); buses also serve the mall.

• Parking & practical access: Compass One has on-site parking with published hours for cars and motorcycles. The parking page lists operating hours as 7:00am–11:00pm daily.

• Best for quick visits & family stops: With groceries, food, library access and many daily-need stores, it works well for a short stop, errands, or a family meal after Sengkang Station.

---

⚠️ What to keep in mind

• Store hours vary: Not every shop follows the mall’s main opening hours; some open much earlier for breakfast or close sooner on holidays. Check the tenant page if you are going for a specific store.
• Holiday changes: The mall publishes festive operating hours and some stores may close or shift timings during major holidays.
• Parking charges apply: Parking is paid and has no grace period, so short visits can still incur a charge.
• Facilities are useful but simple: The mall offers customer service on Level 3 and free Wi-Fi with membership perks, but it is still mainly a neighbourhood mall rather than a luxury destination.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Compass 1 shopping mall linked directly to mrt station n Lrt station... Lots to shop like miniso, clothing shops,Japan home,value shop ... Lots to eat ... Kopitiam with wide range of food options from halal to non halal ... Food for everyone to eat ... Fast food options such as McDonalds,kfc, burger king,mos burger,popoyes ... All at compass 1 ...
Bun shops,cakes,old change kee,Mr bean also at compass 1 ... There is a ananas food shop at just outside the entrance to compass 1 which has dine in options... Also a Muslim shop right beside it which is a takeaway place ... Boost,fruit juice shop,pizza,stuff'd and lots more food at compass 1 ... Weekdays r usually less crowded compared to weekends...

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: **Compass One** is a well-planned neighbourhood mall directly linked to Sengkang MRT and bus interchange.
